what_it_does: Deterministic, multi-language structural extractor that scans a repository tree, parses Python via AST and other languages via regex, emits file/symbol/edge records with signals, resolves edges in memory, and bulk-loads the result into a Supabase ledger via psql COPY.
observations: Only deterministic facts are emitted; model fields are intentionally left for a later interpretive pass.
Reuses code_archaeology.enumerate_files and the same read/secret/binary exclusion gates.
Symbols use path-based code_ids to avoid stem collisions across the whole tree.
Registry-row detection is open: any module-level uppercase dict assignment is captured.
Extractors are stored in a versioned registry list; adding a language means adding a row.
Deep extraction is skipped for oversized or minified files and recorded as a shallow node with a reason.
Edge resolution happens in memory before DB load using same-file > import > unambiguous-global precedence.
Supabase load uses psql COPY with env-overridable PGCONF defaults.
Excluded paths (discovery scratch, recollection archives, separate claude-ds project) are recorded with reasons.
The TS/TSX/JS extractor is regex-based and notes a future AST upgrade.
Markdown extraction captures headings, links, wikilinks, and frontmatter keys while leaving prose meaning to the model layer.
JSON files are classified by role (config/registry/data) and scanned for scheme refs only when modest in size.
Shell extraction recognizes function definitions, exported env vars, and invocations of known binaries.
Systemd extraction parses INI sections and emits a references edge for WantedBy.
SQL extraction identifies tables, columns, indexes, policies, functions, triggers, and extensions.
standouts: In-memory edge resolution with explicit precedence before persistence.
Path-based symbol IDs fixing stem collisions.
Open scheme detection via regex with a registered-scheme flag.
conventions: Heavy module docstring with run examples and design decisions.
Internal helpers are prefixed with underscore.
Python uses AST walking; other languages use line-oriented regex scanners.
Configuration is env-overridable via a PGCONF dict.
Registry-philosophy: adding a language adds a row, not an if/elif.
concerns: The TS/JS regex extractor can miss multiline imports, generic types, and nested declarations.
Shell function regex only matches the 'name()' form and misses alternative syntaxes.
_too_big_to_deep_parse materializes all lines to find max line length despite the comment about avoiding it.
resolve_edges resolves calls by global name uniqueness, which can misresolve common names.
current_hashes re-reads every file, duplicating _read logic and I/O.
PGCONF contains hardcoded default credentials in source.
_EMIT_FNS is a small hardcoded set and may miss other event-emitting patterns.
extract_python's lazy import flag uses col_offset > 0, which can misclassify indented top-level imports.
notes: A grown, versioned, whole-tree structural scanner that keeps deterministic extraction separate from interpretive/model layers and loads a non-flat ledger.
questions: What is the exact Supabase ledger schema shape that load_run expects?
How are resolved edges validated after a load beyond the health_check counts?
What triggers re-runs and how does staleness detection handle moved or renamed files?
Is the regex-based TS extractor accurate enough for the project's TypeScript files?
How are model-generated fields later merged back into existing ledger rows?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches closely: it claims to be a deterministic whole-tree extractor that writes the non-flat Supabase ledger, and the code implements extraction, edge resolution, and psql COPY loading.
apparent_themes: Multi-project ledger with an address spine.
intention_signals: Build a single, trustworthy structural map of the entire codebase (files, symbols, edges, signals) that downstream interpretive/model layers can query and enrich, while avoiding derived-artifact noise and keeping extraction deterministic and reproducible.
novelty: The combination of path-based symbol IDs, open scheme detection, a versioned extractor registry, in-memory edge resolution with explicit precedence, and shallow-node treatment of minified/oversized files in one build tool.
contribution: A deterministic, multi-language extraction pipeline that produces versioned file/symbol/edge records, resolves references before persistence, and loads them into a ledger while recording exclusions honestly.
summary_for_embedding: ledger_build.py is a deterministic repository-wide structural extractor that parses Python AST and regex-scans other languages to emit file, symbol, and edge records for a non-flat Supabase ledger.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a reproducible, model-free structural substrate that a fused system can enrich with interpretive layers.

symbols.does: Returns the current UTC timestamp as an ISO-8601 string.
symbols.description: Small utility used to stamp extraction and load events with a consistent timestamp format.

symbols.does: Reads a file as text after applying sensitive/binary/log exclusion gates.
symbols.description: Mirrors the code_archaeology read gates and returns source plus raw bytes, or a reason string when excluded.

symbols.does: Converts an AST node back to source text, falling back to empty string.
symbols.description: Used throughout Python extraction to capture annotations, decorators, defaults, and base classes as readable strings.

symbols.does: Returns a list of decorator source strings for an AST node.
symbols.description: Wraps _unparse over decorator_list so symbol records can store decorator metadata.

symbols.does: Builds a structured parameter/return signature dict from a function AST.
symbols.description: Handles positional-only, positional, vararg, keyword-only, and kwarg parameters, including annotations and defaults.

symbols.does: Appends one parameter entry to the signature dict.
symbols.description: Local helper inside _signature that normalizes parameter name, annotation, default, and kind.

symbols.does: Computes maximum nesting depth and branch count over an AST body.
symbols.description: AST visitor that increments depth for nesting constructs and counts branch nodes to produce structural signals.

symbols.does: Initializes depth counters for the AST visitor.
symbols.description: Sets maxd, cur, and branches to zero before traversal.

symbols.does: Recursively visits children while tracking nesting depth and branch constructs.
symbols.description: Visits block and definition nodes to update max depth, and counts if/for/while/try/with branches.

symbols.does: Collects raw call targets inside an AST node body.
symbols.description: Returns (target, line) pairs for Name and Attribute calls; used to emit calls edges from symbols.

symbols.does: Deep deterministic extraction of one Python file.
symbols.description: Parses the source with ast, walks imports, module-level constants/dicts, all function/class definitions, env-var accesses, routes, events, raises, scheme refs, and structural depth, returning symbols and edges.

symbols.does: Builds a path-based code_id for a symbol.
symbols.description: Local helper that prefixes the symbol qualified name with the full file path to avoid collisions.

symbols.does: Recursively walks AST nodes to collect all function/class symbols.
symbols.description: Handles nested and conditional definitions, distinguishes methods from functions, and emits per-symbol calls edges.

symbols.does: Finds TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK markers, stub words, and __main__ blocks.
symbols.description: Returns a capped list of marker records with line and surrounding text; used across all extractors.

symbols.does: Heuristically decides whether an import target is internal to the project.
symbols.description: Checks the top-level module name against a hardcoded set of project packages or relative imports.

symbols.does: Returns the filename stem of a relative path.
symbols.description: Used to build the legacy stem_id alias for symbols.

symbols.does: Renders a human-readable function signature string.
symbols.description: Combines parameter names, annotations, defaults, and return annotation into a single-line signature.

symbols.does: Converts a character position into a 1-based line number.
symbols.description: Shared helper used by regex-based extractors to report line locations.

symbols.does: Finds all scheme:// strings and flags registered company schemes.
symbols.description: Uses an open regex and compares against contracts.address.SCHEMES when available.

symbols.does: Regex-based extraction for TypeScript/TSX/JavaScript files.
symbols.description: Scans line by line for imports, function/class/type declarations, arrow functions, exports, API calls, and events; notes a future AST upgrade.

symbols.does: Appends a symbol record for a TS/JS declaration.
symbols.description: Local helper that normalizes code_id, stem_id, kind, signature, and export status.

symbols.does: Regex-based extraction for SQL files.
symbols.description: Identifies tables, columns, indexes, policies, functions, triggers, extensions, and migration order; emits table symbols and references.

symbols.does: Parses systemd unit/timer files into sections and key values.
symbols.description: Extracts Description, ExecStart, WantedBy, After, Requires, OnCalendar, and emits a references edge to WantedBy.

symbols.does: Returns the first value for a section/key in parsed systemd config.
symbols.description: Local helper used to pull single-valued fields like ExecStart.

symbols.does: Regex-based extraction for shell scripts.
symbols.description: Finds function definitions, exported environment variables, and invocations of known binaries; emits symbols and calls edges.

symbols.does: Extracts shape and role metadata from JSON files.
symbols.description: Parses JSON, classifies role (config/registry/data), captures top-level keys, notes generated files, and scans modest files for scheme refs.

symbols.does: Extracts structure from Markdown files.
symbols.description: Parses YAML frontmatter, headings, links, wikilinks, and scheme references; leaves prose meaning to the model layer.

symbols.does: Light extraction for CSS files.
symbols.description: Collects custom CSS property names, counts rules, and detects :root.

symbols.does: Light extraction for HTML files.
symbols.description: Finds data-ui-ref attributes, title, and external script sources; emits references edges for UI refs.

symbols.does: Records shape of JSONL data files without parsing all content.
symbols.description: Counts non-empty lines and captures keys from the first record.

symbols.does: Wraps extract_python and moves several lists into extra_fields.
symbols.description: Adapter used by the extractor registry to keep the top-level record shape consistent.

symbols.does: Decides whether a file should be shallow-parsed only.
symbols.description: Checks total size and max line length to avoid minified/generated files; returns a reason string or None.

symbols.does: Dispatches a file record to the appropriate extractor or shallow fallback.
symbols.description: Reads the file, chooses an extractor by extension/language, runs extraction, and adds base metadata.

symbols.does: Returns an environment dict with PGPASSWORD set for psql.
symbols.description: Local helper for subprocess psql calls.

symbols.does: Builds the base psql command list from PGCONF.
symbols.description: Includes host, port, user, db, and ON_ERROR_STOP flag.

symbols.does: Runs a single psql SQL command and returns trimmed text output.
symbols.description: Raises RuntimeError on nonzero exit; used for schema queries and small commands.

symbols.does: Returns the current git HEAD SHA or 'unknown'.
symbols.description: Used to tag ledger runs with repository version.

symbols.does: Serializes a value to a JSON string.
symbols.description: Helper for CSV loading of JSON-typed columns.

symbols.does: Writes rows to a CSV file with minimal quoting.
symbols.description: Used to stage bulk COPY files for psql loading.

symbols.does: Fetches {path: source_hash} from the latest ledger run.
symbols.description: Queries ledger.latest_run and ledger.entry to establish a staleness baseline.

symbols.does: Computes current source hashes for the whole tree.
symbols.description: Uses enumerate_files and _read; marks scratch-prefix files with an empty hash to align with excluded entries.

symbols.does: Fills to_resolved for every edge using in-memory facts.
symbols.description: Applies precedence contains > imports > same-file calls/extends > unambiguous global; records resolution count in stats.

symbols.does: Extracts the file path portion from a code_id reference.
symbols.description: Local helper used during edge resolution to look up same-file symbols.

symbols.does: Returns the exclusion reason for a path prefix or None.
symbols.description: Maps EXCLUDE_PREFIXES to reasons so excluded files are recorded honestly.

symbols.does: Extracts every file under a folder (or whole tree) plus folder nodes and contains edges.
symbols.description: Builds entries, symbols, edges, and stats deterministically without touching the database.

symbols.does: Bulk-loads an extraction result into the Supabase ledger.
symbols.description: Creates a run record, writes CSVs, and uses psql COPY to populate ledger tables; returns the run_id.

symbols.does: Emits a legacy-format graph payload from the latest ledger run.
symbols.description: Queries ledger.entry and ledger.symbol to produce symbols/edges compatible with older consumers.

symbols.does: Ensures a value is a non-empty string, defaulting to empty.
symbols.description: Local helper used while normalizing legacy symbol rows.

symbols.does: Runs basic ledger consistency counts after a load.
symbols.description: Compares expected vs actual row counts and returns a boolean pass/fail.

symbols.does: Executes a count query and returns an integer.
symbols.description: Local helper inside health_check that parses psql integer output.

symbols.does: CLI entry point for show/folder/load/health/legacy commands.
symbols.description: Parses arguments, dispatches extraction or load, and prints results.
